name: Amari
bio:
Amari is a bright and energetic 10-year-old with a huge love for sports. He enjoys playing flag football, shooting hoops, watching WWE, and hanging out with friends in his neighborhood. His energy is contagious, and his competitive spirit lights up every field and court he's on. One morning on the way to school, what started as an ordinary drive turned into a moment that changed everything. A car traveling the wrong way struck Amari and his father head-on. The accident left Amari with serious injuries, including paralysis and the loss of his right leg. Since that day, Amari and his family have been adjusting to a new way of life as he learns to navigate the world in a wheelchair. Sunshine on a Ranney Day is honored to be part of Amari's journey. SOARD will transforming Amari's home into a space that supports his growth and independence, complete with a wheelchair-accessible bathroom, widened doorways, and a dream bedroom. This renovation is more than construction, it's the foundation for Amari's continued growth, independence, and endless possibilities ahead.


In April 2025, former Atlanta Hawks player Trae Young met 9-year-old Amari in the hospital. Amari’s life changed forever after a head-on collision on the way to school with his dad. Amari was left paralyzed and lost his leg. In a moment, everything changed for him and his family. Trae wanted to do more than visit. He wanted to make a real difference. That’s when he reached out to Sunshine on a Ranney Day. With help from the talented Crosby Design Group, we designed and built Amari a wheelchair-accessible bathroom and a dream bedroom. These new spaces help make daily life easier and allow Amari to regain some independence that was stripped from him following the accident. We feel grateful that both Trae and Amari were involved in every step of the process and excited show you what the past few months have been like as we brought this project to life.


The SOARD team and Amari’s family joined Trae at his pickleball tournament benefiting the Trae Young Family Foundation, a fun-filled night for an incredible cause. In October 2025, Trae met back up with the SOARD team and Amari’s family at Walk Your Plans Atlanta, where Amari got to see his wheelchair-accessible bathroom and dream bedroom come to life, full scale. Huge thank you to the Walk Your Plans Atlanta team for making moments like this possible!

On Demo Day, the awesome team from Kids R Kids Corporate jumped in alongside Amari and our project manager Christian to gut the bathroom and bedroom and kick off construction. Hammers were swinging, walls were coming down, and a few folks were having way too much fun!

This reveal went national! NBC Nightly News with Tony Llamas came out to film a special story on Amari’s makeover, and it aired nationwide the very next day.

Amari’s parents requested his bedroom stay upstairs so he wouldn’t feel separated from the rest of the house, something we understood right away. Trae made it possible for Amari to receive a stair lift, ensuring he can safely and comfortably access his room every day. While SOARD doesn’t install stair lifts, the incredible team at 101 Mobility stepped in to make it happen.

Jennifer Crosby of Crosby Design Group, along with junior designer Ella Guinn, completely reimagined the bathroom layout to provide Amari more room to move, turn easily, and safely use the shower Amari shares the bathroom with his sister, so plenty of storage was added, making the space work for both of them.

Time and time again, our partners show up, and this project was no exception. The same incredible companies who step in for every SOARD makeover immediately raised their hands to help make Amari’s makeover possible. Mohawk Flooring provided the new flooring for Amari’s room, MSI supplied the tile for his bathroom, CR Home and Cambria the vanity countertops, and our trusted partner Real Floors Commercial continually goes above and beyond, donating the Schluter system and all of the labor for both the flooring and tile. These are the partnerships that make our work possible. We truly could not change lives without partners who show up, project after project, with generosity and heart.

Crosby Design Group packed Amari’s bedroom with incredible custom details, including a one-of-a-kind bed designed to look like a shoebox and a built-in closet and desk made just for him. Mike Kamber and the team at Therapy Gyms brought the custom bed to life, while Echols Glass & Mirror crafted the fully custom closet and desk that completes the space.


We are deeply grateful to Trae Young and the Trae Young Family Foundation, Crosby Design Group, and every partner who showed up for Amari and his family. Because of you, Amari now has a home where he can heal, grow, and regain his independence, and his family can move forward with a little more ease, comfort, and hope.
